name: update_sheet_values
description: Write a 2D array of values into a range of a Google Spreadsheet. Values are entered with USER_ENTERED parsing (strings are interpreted as if typed in the UI — formulas, dates and numbers are parsed). Use read_sheet (no range) first to discover sheet titles and dimensions.

spreadsheet_id.description: The Google Sheets spreadsheet ID to write into.

range.description: A1-notation range to write (e.g. "Sheet1!A1" — the top-left anchor; values fill down/right from there).

values.description: A 2D array of cell values: an array of rows, each row an array of cell values (strings/numbers/formulas). E.g. [["Name", "Total"], ["Widget", "=B2*1.1"]].

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

returns: JSON with updated_range and updated_cells (or an error string).
notes: valueInputOption is USER_ENTERED, so a string like "=SUM(A1:A5)" becomes a formula and "5/1/2026" becomes a date. For structural edits (add/delete sheets, formatting, merges) use sheets_batch_update instead.
